popularStory.headline: Adelaide di Susa (d 1091) Bio Sketch
popularStory.description:
BIO:  heiress of Turin and Susa.

** from http://fmg.ac/Projects/MedLands/NORTHERN%20ITALY%20900-1100.htm#AdelaidaSusadied1091, as of 10/29/2014

zh.lifeSummary: 當Adelaide di Susa於1014年出生在義大利皮蒙特托利諾蘇薩時，她的父親Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa36歲，母親Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi39歲。她於1028年之前與Otton de Fézensac在法國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1091年12月19日在法國薩伏伊享年77歲，埋葬在義大利皮蒙特托利諾卡尼奧。

nameMeanings.name: di
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mandarin form of the surname 狄, a term referring to the tribes in ancient northern China: (i) from Di (狄), the name of an ancient state (thought to have been located in Hebei or Shandong province), which was granted to Xiao Bo or Kao Bo by King Cheng of Zhou (reigned 1042–1021  BC ). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Ku Di (庫狄) family during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) traced back to other minority ethnic groups in ancient northwestern China.

Mandarin form of the Chinese surname 邸, meaning ‘official residence’ in Chinese: (i) from placename Di (邸), the name of a county (unidentified location). (ii) said to be borne by some descendants of Kujula Kadphises (called 丘就郤 or 邸就郤 in Chinese, 30–c. 80 AD ), founder of the Kushan Empire (one branch of the Yuezhi people originating from western China) located in Central Asia during the Eastern Han dynasty (25–220 AD ).

variants.name: Chi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Dong, Yun, Cheng, Jae, Yong, Cheng Chung, Chi, Chun, Duk, Fang, Feng.  Korean Chung, Chong, Jae Hong, Moon, Myong, Pong, Shen, Yoo, Byung, Chang, Chol, Chong Hun.  Southeast Asian Phi, Hung, Ly, Tu, Denh, Do Van, Duong, Hao, Kieu, Mui, Nga, Phat.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 池, meaning ‘moat’ in ancient Chinese: (i) acquired as a surname by people who lived beside a moat. (ii) from Chi (池), said to be the name of an ancient state (of unknown location and period). (iii) from the personal name Chi (池), personal name of a son of an official in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 遲, meaning ‘late’ in Chinese: (i) borne by descendants of Chi Ren (遲任), a person who lived during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the Chinese compound surname Yu-Chi (尉遲), originally the name of a tribe from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iii) from the second element of the personal name Zi Chi (子遲), style name of Fan Xu, a student of Confucius (551–479  BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Susa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Serbian and Croatian (Šuša): nickname from šuša ‘cattle without horns’, also ‘small, insignificant person’.

Slovenian, Croatian, Serbian, and Bosniak (Suša, Slovenian also Šuša): nickname for a very thin man, derived from Slovenian, Croatian, and Bosnian suh, Serbian suv ‘thin’ or, in Slovenian, from its derivative suša (dialect šuša) ‘scraggy or scrawny fellow’.
Czech: from a short form of the personal name Susana (Czech Zuzana), or a nickname from Latin sus ‘pig’.

nameMeanings.name: Adelaide
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic origin (via French Adélaïde ), from adal ‘noble’ + heid ‘kind, sort’. It was borne in the 10th century by the wife of the Holy Roman Emperor Otto the Great. She became regent after his death and was revered as a saint. The given name increased in popularity in England during the 19th century, when it was borne by the wife of King William IV; she was the daughter of the ruler of the German duchy of Saxe-Meiningen. The Australian city of Adelaide was named in her honour.

en.lifeSummary: When Adelaide di Susa was born in 1014, in Susa, Turin, Piedmont, Italy, her father, Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa, was 36 and her mother, Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi, was 39. She married Otton de Fézensac before 1028, in France. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 19 December 1091, in Savoie, France, at the age of 77, and was buried in Canischio, Turin, Piedmont, Italy.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adelaide di Susa est née en 1014, à Susa, Torino, Piemonte, Italie, son père, Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi, avait 39 ans. Elle a épousé Otton de Fézensac avant 1028, à France. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 19 décembre 1091, à Savoie, France, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Canischio, Torino, Piemonte, Italie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adelaide di Susa 1014 in Susa, Torino, Piemonte, Italien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa 36 und ihre Mutter Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi 39. Sie hat vor 1028 in Frankreich Otton de Fézensac geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 19. Dezember 1091 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Savoyen, Frankreich und wurde in Canischio, Torino, Piemonte, Italien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adelaide di Susa è nata il nel 1014 nel Susa, Torino, Piemonte, Italia, sua padre, Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa, aveva 36 anni e sua madre, Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi, aveva 39 anni. Lei sposato Otton de Fézensac prima del 1028, nel Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 19 dicembre 1091, nel Savoia, Francia, all'età di 77 anni, e fu sepolta a Canischio, Torino, Piemonte, Italia.

ko.San Giovanni Battista, Torino, Torino, P: 이탈리아 피몽 토리노 토리노 산 조반이 바티스타
ko.lifeSummary: Adelaide di Susa(이)가 1014년 이탈리아 피몽 토리노 수사에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi(은)는 39세였다. 그녀는 1028년 이전 프랑스에서 Otton de Fézensac(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1091년 12월 19일 프랑스 서아보이에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 이탈리아 피몽 토리노 캔이시오에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adelaide di Susa nasceu em 1014, em Susa, Torino, Piemonte, Itália, seu pai, Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi, tinha 39 anos. Ela casou-se com Otton de Fézensac antes 1028, em França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 19 de dezembro de 1091, em Savoie, França, com 77 anos, e foi sepultada em Canischio, Torino, Piemonte, Itália.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adelaide di Susa родилась в 1014 г. в местности сьюса, торино, пиемонт, Италия, её отцу по имени Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi, 39. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Otton de Fézensac перед 1028 г. в местности Франция. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 19 декабря 1091 г. в местности савои, Франция в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности канищио, торино, пиемонт, Италия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adelaide di Susa nació en 1014, en Susa, Torino, Piemonte, Italia, su papá, Olderico Manfredi II marchese di Torino e Susa, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Bertha d'Ivrea of Milan of the Obertenghi, tenía 39 años. Se casó con Otton de Fézensac antes de 1028, en Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 19 de diciembre de 1091, en Savoie, Francia, a la edad de 77 años, y fue sepultada en Canischio, Torino, Piemonte, Italia.
